**Jever** is a German [beer](/wiki/Beer "Beer") brand, named after the town of [Jever](/wiki/Jever "Jever") where it is brewed. It has been brewed by the "Friesisches Brauhaus zu Jever" (the Jever Frisian [Brewery](/wiki/Brewery "Brewery")) since 1848.

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Jever_Brewery&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/b/b9/Friesischesbrauhauszujever2.jpg/250px-Friesischesbrauhauszujever2.jpg)](/wiki/File:Friesischesbrauhauszujever2.jpg)

The Jever Friesian Brewery

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/b/bd/Jever_Bottle.jpg/250px-Jever_Bottle.jpg)](/wiki/File:Jever_Bottle.jpg)

A bottle of Jever

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/9/96/Aus_dem_Friesischen_Brauhaus_zu_Jever_%281%29.JPG/250px-Aus_dem_Friesischen_Brauhaus_zu_Jever_%281%29.JPG)](/wiki/File:Aus_dem_Friesischen_Brauhaus_zu_Jever_(1).JPG)

Jever beer glass

When the "Friesisches Brauhaus zu Jever" was founded by Diedrich König in 1848, it was only one of several breweries in the region. König, however, was convinced from the beginning that his beer was something special. After his death in 1867, his son sold the brewery. Theodor Fetköter took over the brewery. He began by developing the small family business into a large brewery operation, started to advertise and developed special bottles. In 1848, he played an important part in installing the first water supply system in Jever.

The [First World War](/wiki/World_War_I "World War I") was a difficult time for the brewery. Theodor Fetköter's son, who had taken over the business, was killed at the front. The brewery was sold once again, in 1922, to the [Bavaria – St. Pauli Brewery](/wiki/Bavaria_%E2%80%93_St._Pauli_Brewery "Bavaria – St. Pauli Brewery"), based in [Hamburg](/wiki/Hamburg "Hamburg"). Today the Bavaria – St. Pauli Brewery is owned by the [Danish](/wiki/Denmark "Denmark") [Carlsberg Group](/wiki/Carlsberg_Group "Carlsberg Group"). By then, beer from Jever had become known outside the region where it was produced. Since 1934, it has been sold under the name "Jever [Pilsener](/wiki/Pilsener "Pilsener")".

After [World War II](/wiki/World_War_II "World War II"), the brewery once again found itself experiencing difficult times. Due to a fuel shortage, beer could only be sold from the brewery gates directly to customers. It was also necessary to scour the region's farms for malt.

After the post-war period, the situation in Jever improved once again. The top-selling beer was "Jever Export", which was brewed until 1990. During the 1960s, Pilsener beer became more and more popular and sales figures for Jever Pilsener increased. During this period the brewery was expanded.

Since 1994, the brewery has belonged to the Dortmunder [Brau und Brunnen](/wiki/Brau_und_Brunnen "Brau und Brunnen") Group, which was acquired by [Radeberger Group](/wiki/Radeberger_Group "Radeberger Group"), at the beginning of 2005.

## Sponsorships

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Jever_Brewery&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Sponsorships")]

From 2002 to 2005, professional [football](/wiki/Association_football "Association football") club [Borussia Mönchengladbach](/wiki/Borussia_M%C3%B6nchengladbach "Borussia Mönchengladbach") was sponsored by Jever.
